messages.content: Recommend some papers with a strong performance in the task 3D scene reconstruction.

### Classic / foundational (multi-view stereo & SLAM)
- **Newcombe et al., 2011 — “KinectFusion: Real-Time Dense Surface Mapping and Tracking”**  
  A landmark real-time TSDF fusion system for RGB-D 3D reconstruction; set the stage for many fusion-based pipelines.
- **Curless & Levoy, 1996 — “A Volumetric Method for Building Complex Models from Range Images”**  
  The original TSDF-style volumetric fusion idea behind a lot of modern dense reconstruction.
- **Schönberger & Frahm, 2016 — “Structure-from-Motion Revisited” (COLMAP)**  
  Extremely strong classical SfM pipeline widely used as a backbone for recon; robust and accurate.
- **Kazhdan et al., 2006/2013 — “Poisson Surface Reconstruction”**  
  A core meshing method still used to turn point clouds into watertight surfaces.

---

### Neural rendering → high-quality 3D reconstruction (NeRF family)
- **Mildenhall et al., 2020 — “NeRF: Representing Scenes as Neural Radiance Fields for View Synthesis”**  
  Started the neural scene reconstruction wave; strong geometry in many settings (though optimized per-scene).
- **Müller et al., 2022 — “Instant Neural Graphics Primitives (Instant-NGP)”**  
  Hash-grid encoding gives fast training and very competitive reconstruction quality; hugely influential in

I can recommend recent papers, but I don’t currently have reliable access to publications **after 2025‑07** in my offline knowledge. If you want, I can still do one of these effectively:

1) **You tell me the venue(s)** (e.g., SIGGRAPH 2025/2026, CVPR/ICCV/ECCV 2025, 3DV 2025) and I’ll suggest what to look for + keywords/track names and typical “top” papers in those areas.  
2) **Paste titles/abstracts/links** you’ve found, and I’ll shortlist the strongest ones for 3D reconstruction and summarize/compare them.  
3) I can give a **curated list up to 2025‑07** (still very strong) and explain which lines of work are currently winning (3DGS, neural implicit SDFs, feed-forward recon, SLAM).

To tailor a post‑2025‑07 list, please answer two quick questions:
- Are you focusing on **single-image**, **multi-view**, **RGB‑D**, or **LiDAR** reconstruction?
- Output type: **mesh**, **point cloud**, **implicit SDF**, or **radiance field / Gaussian splats**?

If you want something actionable right now, here are **search targets/keywords** that reliably surface the best post‑2025‑07 work in 3D scene reconstruction:
- “**3D Gaussian Splatting** reconstruction” / “**dynamic Gaussian splatting**” / “**SLAM Gaussian splatting**”
- “**feed-forward** multi-view reconstruction” / “**transformer** MVS” / “**foundation model** 3D reconstruction”
- “**neural SDF** scene reconstruction” / “**NeRF to mesh**” / “**regularized SDF**”
- “**open-vocabulary** 3D scene reconstruction” / “**semantic** reconstruction”
- “**large-scale** urban reconstruction” / “**NeRF/GS SLAM**”

And the most relevant venues/tracks where these appear:
- **CVPR / ICCV / ECCV** (3D Vision, Neural Rendering, SLAM)
- **SIGGRAPH / SIGGRAPH Asia** (Neural rendering, reconstruction)
- **3DV** (reconstruction-heavy)
- **RA-L / ICRA / IROS** (SLAM + reconstruction)

If you share 5–10 candidate links (arXiv or proceedings), I’ll rank them by reconstruction quality, scalability, runtime, supervision needs, and whether they produce accurate geometry (vs. only good rendering).
